Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search Linear using linear_list_issues (project: "expense_autocategorizer" or query: "expense" or similar). Discovers all 20 tickets (ENG-201 through ENG-220) with current statuses.
Key findings: ENG-205/206/207/208/209 show state_todo, ENG-211/212/214/215/216/217/218 show state_in_progress, ENG-213 is state_backlog, ENG-219/220 are state_todo.
OE2: Read Linear comments using linear_list_comments (issueId per ticket). Discovers true status evidence from comments.
Key findings: (a) ENG-211 has Lena's RCA, Samira's remediation plan, and Elena's CEO directive. (b) ENG-217 has Dmitri's audit results (47 reviewed, 39 correct, 5 false rejections Vectral, 3 false approvals Mosaic). (c) ENG-216 has Dmitri's duplicate receipt hash findings (Kevin Tran and Andre Washington). (d) ENG-215 has Lena's deploy confirmation for both Vectral and Mosaic config fixes. (e) ENG-202 has Lena's note confirming ENG-205/206/207 complete. (f) ENG-203 has Anh's note confirming ENG-208 and dashboard complete.
OE3: Search Slack #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_search_messages and/or conversations_history (query: "expense" or "relaunch" or similar). Discovers project discussions revealing real status.
Key findings: (a) Samira's go/no-go call scheduled Monday 2pm PT. (b) Chloe's bug report — PivotPoint config exclusion list saved as string instead of array, matching ENG-218 bug. (c) Team discussion threads on false approvals, RCA, remediation. (d) Lena's March 5 progress update confirming ENG-205/206/207/209 all complete, contradicting their state_todo status in Linear.
OE4: Search emails using search_emails (query: "expense" or "relaunch" or "bulk export" or similar). Discovers external blockers not visible in Linear/Slack.
Key findings: (a) Marcus's email flagging CSV bulk export (ENG-213) as a hard dependency for Q2 close and SOX compliance. (b) Lena's email confirming ENG-217 deployed to staging, noting Canopy Health config fails new validation. (c) Mina's email warning not to reactivate for Vectral until contract renegotiation resolves (10% loyalty discount in negotiation); Mosaic timing depends on Emeka re: prototype damage.
OE5: Check Airtable stipend records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions"). Discovers false approvals still showing "Approved" despite Dmitri's audit.
Key records: rec_stipend_andre_desk / recStip000000021 (Andre Washington, desk $1,200, exceeds $800 limit), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington, coworking $350, excluded category), rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork / recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura, coworking $135, excluded category). Duplicate record pairs exist for the same underlying false approvals.
OE6: Look up Samira Tariq using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira" or "Tariq"). Confirms email address samira.tariq@moveops.com before sending the summary email.
OE7: Update stale Linear tickets using linear_update_issue and/or linear_create_comment. Corrects statuses that don't match actual progress.
Updates: ENG-205/206/207 (state_todo → done, per ENG-202 comment + Slack), ENG-208 (state_todo → done, per ENG-203 comment), ENG-209 (state_todo → done, per Lena's March 5 Slack update and Samira's project summary), ENG-214/215 (in_progress → done, per ENG-215 deploy comment), ENG-216 (in_progress → done, per ENG-217 audit results comment). Also adds blocker comments where external dependencies exist (e.g., ENG-213 now a finance hard dependency per Marcus's email).
OE8: Update Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Changes Approval Status from "Approved" to "Pending" or "Rejected" for false approvals flagged by Dmitri's audit.
Records: rec_stipend_andre_desk, recStip000000021 (desk $1,200), recStip000000022 (coworking $350), rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork, recStip000000023 (coworking $135).
OE9: Post readiness summary in #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_add_message. Covers ticket statuses, blockers, Airtable fixes, and overall relaunch readiness before Monday's go/no-go call.
OE10: Send email to Samira (samira.tariq@moveops.com) using send_email. Includes ticket-by-ticket status, blockers discovered, Airtable corrections made, and client-side dependencies flagged (Vectral contract renegotiation per Mina, Mosaic prototype damage situation per Emeka).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE1: Search Linear using linear_list_issues (project: "expense_autocategorizer" or query: "expense" or similar). Discovers all 20 tickets (ENG-201 through ENG-220) with current statuses.
Key findings: ENG-205/206/207/208/209 show state_todo, ENG-211/212/214/215/216/217/218 show state_in_progress, ENG-213 is state_backlog, ENG-219/220 are state_todo.
OE2: Read Linear comments using linear_list_comments (issueId per ticket). Discovers true status evidence from comments.
Key findings: (a) ENG-211 has Lena's RCA, Samira's remediation plan, and Elena's CEO directive. (b) ENG-217 has Dmitri's audit results (47 reviewed, 39 correct, 5 false rejections Vectral, 3 false approvals Mosaic). (c) ENG-216 has Dmitri's duplicate receipt hash findings (Kevin Tran and Andre Washington). (d) ENG-215 has Lena's deploy confirmation for both Vectral and Mosaic config fixes. (e) ENG-202 has Lena's note confirming ENG-205/206/207 complete. (f) ENG-203 has Anh's note confirming ENG-208 and dashboard complete.
OE3: Search Slack #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_search_messages and/or conversations_history (query: "expense" or "relaunch" or similar). Discovers project discussions revealing real status.
Key findings: (a) Samira's go/no-go call scheduled Monday 2pm PT. (b) Chloe's bug report — PivotPoint config exclusion list saved as string instead of array, matching ENG-218 bug. (c) Team discussion threads on false approvals, RCA, remediation. (d) Lena's March 5 progress update confirming ENG-205/206/207/209 all complete, contradicting their state_todo status in Linear.
OE4: Search emails using search_emails (query: "expense" or "relaunch" or "bulk export" or similar). Discovers external blockers not visible in Linear/Slack.
Key findings: (a) Marcus's email flagging CSV bulk export (ENG-213) as a hard dependency for Q2 close and SOX compliance. (b) Lena's email confirming ENG-217 deployed to staging, noting Canopy Health config fails new validation. (c) Mina's email warning not to reactivate for Vectral until contract renegotiation resolves (10% loyalty discount in negotiation); Mosaic timing depends on Emeka re: prototype damage.
OE5: Check Airtable stipend records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions"). Discovers false approvals still showing "Approved" despite Dmitri's audit.
Key records: rec_stipend_andre_desk / recStip000000021 (Andre Washington, desk $1,200, exceeds $800 limit), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington, coworking $350, excluded category), rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork / recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura, coworking $135, excluded category). Duplicate record pairs exist for the same underlying false approvals.
OE6: Look up Samira Tariq using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira" or "Tariq"). Confirms email address samira.tariq@moveops.com before sending the summary email.
OE7: Update stale Linear tickets using linear_update_issue and/or linear_create_comment. Corrects statuses that don't match actual progress.
Updates: ENG-205/206/207 (state_todo → done, per ENG-202 comment + Slack), ENG-208 (state_todo → done, per ENG-203 comment), ENG-209 (state_todo → done, per Lena's March 5 Slack update and Samira's project summary), ENG-214/215 (in_progress → done, per ENG-215 deploy comment), ENG-216 (in_progress → done, per ENG-217 audit results comment). Also adds blocker comments where external dependencies exist (e.g., ENG-213 now a finance hard dependency per Marcus's email).
OE8: Update Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Changes Approval Status from "Approved" to "Pending" or "Rejected" for false approvals flagged by Dmitri's audit.
Records: rec_stipend_andre_desk, recStip000000021 (desk $1,200), recStip000000022 (coworking $350), rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork, recStip000000023 (coworking $135).
OE9: Post readiness summary in #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_add_message. Covers ticket statuses, blockers, Airtable fixes, and overall relaunch readiness before Monday's go/no-go call.
OE10: Send email to Samira (samira.tariq@moveops.com) using send_email. Includes ticket-by-ticket status, blockers discovered, Airtable corrections made, and client-side dependencies flagged (Vectral contract renegotiation per Mina, Mosaic prototype damage situation per Emeka).
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues) to retrieve expense auto-categorizer tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mcp__linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'expense', and also by project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer'. The tool was used to retrieve expense auto-categorizer tickets.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'expense', 'ENG', and also with project/team parameters (proj_expense_autocategorizer, team_engineering).
✓Run #3The agent called mcp__mcp_91c5570e__linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'expense', and 'ENG', as well as with project/team filters.
✓Run #4The agent called mcp__mcp_aeaf992c__linear_list_issues multiple times with queries like 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'expense', and with project='proj_expense_autocategorizer'. These calls returned the full set of 20 tickets.
✓Run #5The agent called mcp__mcp_0f926c40__linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'expense', 'ENG', and also with project/label parameters.
✓Run #6The agent called mcp__mcp_15d194e4__linear_list_issues multiple times, including with queries 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'expense', 'ENG', and also with project/label parameters. This criterion is clearly met.
2 The model must use a Linear comments tool (e.g., linear_list_comments) to read comments on individual tickets.
tool selection
 ✗✗✓✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent never called linear_list_comments in the trajectory. It gathered ticket context through linear_list_issues (which includes descriptions) and Slack/email searches, but did not explicitly read Linear comments on individual tickets.
✗Run #2No linear_list_comments tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent relied on Slack searches and email searches rather than reading Linear ticket comments to verify actual completion status.
✓Run #3The agent called mcp__mcp_91c5570e__linear_list_comments on issue_pipeline_hardening, issue_edge_cases_backlog, and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection to read comments on individual tickets.
✗Run #4No call to linear_list_comments (or any equivalent) appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent gathered information about ticket completion via other means (Slack searches, email, and inferring from the tickets themselves) but never explicitly called a linear comments tool.
✗Run #5No linear_list_comments (or equivalent) call appears in the trajectory. The agent read Slack threads via conversations_replies but never called a linear_list_comments tool to check comments on individual tickets.
✗Run #6The agent never called linear_list_comments (or any equivalent) at any point in the trajectory. It relied on ticket data from linear_list_issues and cross-referenced with Slack/email searches, but never read comments on individual Linear tickets.
3 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) to check stipend records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mcp__airtable_list_records on base 'appMoveOpsOps001', table 'Stipend Transactions', and received the full list of records including the false approval/rejection records.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_list_records on the 'Stipend Transactions' table in base appMoveOpsOps001, which returned all stipend records including the ones requiring correction.
✓Run #3The agent called mcp__mcp_91c5570e__airtable_list_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' to check stipend records.
✓Run #4The agent called mcp__mcp_aeaf992c__airtable_list_records with base_id='appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name='Stipend Transactions', which returned stipend records.
✓Run #5The agent called mcp__mcp_0f926c40__airtable_list_records on the 'Stipend Transactions' table in the appMoveOpsOps001 base.
✓Run #6The agent called mcp__mcp_15d194e4__airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 with table_name 'Stipend Transactions', satisfying this criterion.
4 The model must pass a query related to "expense" or "categorizer" or "expense_autocategorizer" (or similar) when calling linear_list_issues.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used queries 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', and 'expense' when calling linear_list_issues — all containing 'expense' and/or 'categorizer' as required.
✓Run #2The agent used queries 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', and 'expense' when calling linear_list_issues, all of which contain the required keyword 'expense'.
✓Run #3The agent queried linear_list_issues with 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', and 'expense', all of which are relevant queries for finding the expense auto-categorizer project tickets.
✓Run #4The agent's linear_list_issues calls included queries 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'expense', and a project parameter 'proj_expense_autocategorizer' — all containing 'expense' and/or 'categorizer'.
✓Run #5linear_list_issues was called with queries 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', and 'expense', all matching the required query terms.
✓Run #6The agent passed queries including 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', and 'expense' to linear_list_issues calls, all of which satisfy the requirement for an expense/categorizer-related query.
5 The model must pass a search_query related to "expense" or "relaunch" or "categorizer" or "ExpenseBot" (or similar) when calling conversations_search_messages.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called conversations_search_messages with 'expense auto-categorizer' and 'expense categorizer', both containing 'expense' and 'categorizer', satisfying the OR condition in the criterion.

query construction

✗Run #1The agent did NOT update Andre Washington's standing desk record (recStip000000021 / rec_stipend_andre_desk). The agent's airtable_update_records calls only corrected false rejections (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park), not the false approvals. The agent explicitly stated the Mosaic false approvals were correctly documented per Elena's directive.
✗Run #2The agent only corrected false DENIAL records (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park → Approved). Andre Washington's standing desk record (recStip000000021/rec_stipend_andre_desk) which was a false APPROVAL showing 'Approved' was never updated to Pending or Rejected.
✗Run #3The agent did not correct Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk record. The agent's own response explicitly states 'The Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 desk + $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking) remain marked as Approved per Elena's directive.' The airtable_update_records calls only corrected the 3 Vectral false denial records.
✗Run #4The agent only updated Airtable records for false denials (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) — correcting Rejected→Approved. It did NOT update Andre Washington's standing desk ($1,200) which was a false approval and should be changed from Approved to Pending/Rejected. The agent completely missed correcting the false approvals.
✗Run #5The agent's Airtable updates only corrected false denials (Vectral employees recStip000000018, 19, 20, rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi). Andre Washington's standing desk record (false approval, recStip000000021) was not updated from Approved to Pending/Rejected.
✗Run #6The agent only corrected false rejections (changing records to Approved). It never updated Andre Washington's standing desk record (a false approval) from Approved to Pending/Rejected. Both airtable_update_records calls set records to 'Approved', none changed any record away from Approved.

While Q2 close and SOX aren't named explicitly, Marcus and the finance dependency are clearly referenced.

✓Run #6The email references 'ENG-213 (Bulk Export) — Me, in backlog. Marcus specifically needs this for Mosaic reconciliation.' The criterion uses 'or' logic and explicitly mentions Marcus Thorne and bulk export, which are both present.
